zh.Bethune, Nouvelle-Aquitanne, France: 法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯貝圖內
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux於大約1352年出生在法國時，他的父親Jean de Béthune36歲，母親Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux24歲。他於1368年與Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon結婚。他去世於1408年2月在法國瑟-吉馬內米奧享年57歲。

zh-hans.Bethune, Nouvelle-Aquitanne, France: 法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯貝圖內
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux于大约1352年出生在法国时，他的父亲Jean de Béthune36岁，母亲Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux24岁。他于1368年与Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon结婚。他去世于1408年2月在法国瑟-吉馬內米奧享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Béthune
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin); French (Béthune): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, Picardy, France, recorded in the 8th century in the Latin form Bitunia, probably an adjective (with villa understood) derived from the oblique form of an ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ). See also Scottish Beaton .

variants.name: Beaton
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, France (see Bethune ). The Hebridean family of McBeth , hereditary physicians, adopted the surname Beaton in the 16th century.
English: from the Middle English personal name Beton, a diminutive of Bete, a pet form of Beatrice (see Beatrice ). Compare Beat .

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux was born about 1352, in France, his father, Jean de Béthune, was 36 and his mother, Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux, was 24. He married Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon in 1368. He died in February 1408, in Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, France, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux est né environ 1352, à France, son père, Jean de Béthune, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon en 1368. Il est décédé en février 1408, à Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, France, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux um 1352 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean de Béthune 36 und seine Mutter Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux 24. Er hat 1368 Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon geheiratet. Er starb im Februar 1408 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux è nato il circa 1352 nel Francia, il suo padre, Jean de Béthune, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon nel 1368. Morì nel febbraio 1408, nel Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meauxが約1352年にフランスで生まれた時，父Jean de Béthuneは36歳，母Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meauxは24歳であった。1368年にJeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillonと結婚した。彼は1408年2月にフランスセアイネ-エト-マルンメアウックスで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux(이)가 대략 1352년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean de Béthune(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1368년 Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1408년 2월 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 모스에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux nasceu aproximadamente 1352, em França, seu pai, Jean de Béthune, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon em 1368. Ele faleceu em fevereiro de 1408, em Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, França, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux родился около 1352 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени Jean de Béthune было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon в 1368 г. Он умер февраль 1408 г. в местности мокс, сине-ет-марн, Франция в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Béthune VIII, Viscount of Meaux nació aproximadamente 1352, en Francia, su papá, Jean de Béthune, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Jeanne de Coucy Vicomtesse de Meaux, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Jeanne de Saint-Paul de Chatillon en 1368. Murió en febrero de 1408, en Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, a la edad de 57 años.
